Title: The MdHB7L–MdICE1L–MdHOS1 Module Fine-Tunes Apple Cold Response via CBF-Dependent and CBF-Independent Pathways

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limits excessive ROS accumulation under cold stress and supports cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdHB7L directly activates MdPRX52 expression, promoting antioxidant enzyme activity and reducing ROS under cold stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq/RNA-seq integration; RT-qPCR; Dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; ROS and antioxidant assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under cold; higher in MdHB7L-OE and lower in MdHB7L-RNAi plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdPRX52 was identified as a direct MdHB7L target from ChIP-seq and RNA-seq overlap. 2. MdHB7L binds the MdPRX52 promoter and activates its transcription under cold. 3. Its activation is associated with enhanced ROS scavenging and cold tolerance.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terminal metabolite is not a nutrient metabolite; included because it is a direct core target in the Results but functions in antioxidant metabolism rather than nutritional quality.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Limits excessive ROS accumulation under cold stress and supports cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdHB7L directly activates MdPRX53 expression, promoting antioxidant enzyme activity and reducing ROS under cold stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq/RNA-seq integration; RT-qPCR; Dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; ROS and antioxidant assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under cold; higher in MdHB7L-OE and lower in MdHB7L-RNAi plants
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdPRX53 was identified as a direct MdHB7L target. 2. MdHB7L activates MdPRX53 promoter transcription. 3. This contributes to antioxidant capacity during cold response.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Terminal metabolite is not a nutrient metabolite; included as a direct target involved in metabolic adaptation.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to cold tolerance and antioxidant protection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdHB7L directly activates MdPAP2 and promotes anthocyanin accumulation under cold stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq/RNA-seq integration; RT-qPCR; metabolite measurement; Dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold treatment at 4°C for 35 d under LD conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in MdHB7L-OE and lower in MdHB7L-RNAi under cold
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdPAP2 was identified among MdHB7L direct targets. 2. MdHB7L binds and activates the MdPAP2 promoter. 3. MdPAP2 upregulation is associated with increased anthocyanin accumulation during cold stress.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although MdPAP2 is itself a regulator of anthocyanin accumulation, in this paper it is mainly treated as a downstream target of the core module rather than the primary experimental subject.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as osmoprotectant and supports cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdHB7L directly activates MdERDL6-1 expression and promotes soluble sugar accumulation under cold stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolite measurement; Dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in MdHB7L-OE and lower in MdHB7L-RNAi under cold
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdERDL6-1 was detected as an MdHB7L target. 2. MdHB7L binds and activates the MdERDL6-1 promoter. 3. This regulation is associated with higher soluble sugar accumulation under cold.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a transporter-like ERD6 family member, not an enzyme or TF.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as osmoprotectant and supports cold tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdHB7L directly activates MdERDL6-7 expression and promotes soluble sugar accumulation under cold stress
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq/RNA-seq integration; RT-qPCR; metabolite measurement; Dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in MdHB7L-OE and lower in MdHB7L-RNAi under cold
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdERDL6-7 was identified as a direct MdHB7L target. 2. MdHB7L binds and activates its promoter. 3. This contributes to soluble sugar accumulation during cold stress.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it is a transporter-like ERD6 family member.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Compatible solute/osmoprotectant contributing to cold tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdHB7L represses MdERD5 expression, thereby promoting proline accumulation under cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes proline catabolism, reducing proline accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq/RNA-seq integration; RT-qPCR; metabolite measurement; Dual-LUC; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in MdHB7L-OE and higher in MdHB7L-RNAi under cold
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdERD5 is a direct MdHB7L target linked to proline metabolism. 2. MdHB7L binds the promoter and inhibits its transcription. 3. Repression of MdERD5 is associated with increased proline accumulation under cold.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally connected to proline accumulation via catabolic repression rather than biosynthetic activation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Anthocyanins provide antioxidant/cold-protective functions; soluble sugars and proline act as osmoprotectants during cold stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes apple cold tolerance and increases anthocyanin, soluble sugar, and proline accumulation while also enhancing CBF expression and ROS scavenging
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation or repression; cofactor-assisted activation with MdICE1L; subject to ubiquitin-mediated degradation by MdHOS1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central positive regulator connecting CBF-dependent and CBF-independent metabolic responses to cold
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin accumulation; soluble sugar accumulation; proline metabolism; antioxidant response; cold signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdCBF1; MdCBF3; MdPRX52; MdPRX53; MdRD26; MdPAP2; MdERDL6-1; MdERDL6-7; MdERD5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MdCBF1/3, MdPRX52/53, MdRD26, MdPAP2, MdERDL6-1, and MdERDL6-7; represses MdERD5
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional complex with MdICE1L; interaction with MdHOS1 affects stability and DNA binding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression/RNAi in apple; phenotyping under freezing; Y1H; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; RNA-seq; Dual-LUC; Y2H; pull-down; Co-IP; split-LUC; degradation and ubiquitination assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold treatment at 4°C; freezing with and without cold acclimation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cold-induced, peaking rapidly after 3 h; promoter strongly activated in leaves under cold
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHB7L is induced by cold and positively regulates apple freezing tolerance. 2. It activates MdCBF1/3 directly and also enhances MdICE1L-mediated activation of MdCBFs. 3. It directly regulates downstream metabolic genes controlling ROS scavenging, anthocyanin, soluble sugar, and proline accumulation. 4. Its stability and activity are negatively regulated by MdHOS1, but early in cold response MdICE1L protects MdHB7L from MdHOS1-mediated degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve cold tolerance together with metabolite protection traits; stacking with compatible regulators or weakening MdHOS1 may enhance effectiveness, but stress-focused deployment is most relevant rather than nutritional breeding per se.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a transcription factor alone, but as a cofactor when complexed with MdICE1L on MdCBF promoters.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Indirectly supports cold-protective metabolite accumulation through activation of CBF-dependent cold signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cold tolerance by activating MdCBF1/3 and by protecting MdHB7L from MdHOS1-mediated degradation early during cold response
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; protein interaction-mediated cofactor recruitment; subject to ubiquitin-mediated degradation by MdHOS1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key early cold-response activator that strongly drives MdCBF expression and shapes the timing of MdHB7L action
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: CBF-dependent cold signaling that secondarily affects antioxidant and osmoprotective metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MdCBF1/3; when complexed with MdHB7L, activation is enhanced
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms regulatory complex with MdHB7L on MdCBF promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; pull-down; Co-IP; split-LUC; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; Dual-LUC; ubiquitination and degradation assays
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdICE1L physically interacts with MdHB7L and MdHOS1. 2. MdHB7L enhances MdICE1L binding and transcriptional activation on MdCBF1/3 promoters. 3. MdICE1L inhibits MdHB7L direct binding to HD-Zip sites, so MdHB7L acts as a cofactor in the complex. 4. MdHOS1 preferentially ubiquitinates and degrades MdICE1L during cold response, changing module dynamics over time.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not directly assigned to nutrient metabolite genes, it is a core regulator in the Results that causally controls downstream metabolite-associated cold responses.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Negatively influences cold-protective metabolite accumulation by suppressing MdHB7L function
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates cold tolerance by ubiquitinating and degrading MdICE1L and MdHB7L and by suppressing MdHB7L DNA binding and target-gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Ubiquitin-mediated degradation; inhibition of DNA binding/transcriptional regulation via protein interaction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major negative regulator attenuating MdHB7L-dependent accumulation of cold-protective metabolites and downstream gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cold signaling; indirect control of anthocyanin, soluble sugar, proline, and antioxidant pathways via MdHB7L and MdICE1L
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly inhibits MdCBF1/3, MdPRX52/53, MdRD26, MdPAP2, MdERDL6-1, MdERDL6-7, and MdERD5 regulation by suppressing MdHB7L
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Competes within MdHB7L–MdICE1L–MdHOS1 module; preferentially binds MdICE1L over MdHB7L
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H/Y3H; split-LUC; Co-IP; pull-down; transgenic overexpression/RNAi; in vitro and in vivo ubiquitination assays; protein degradation assays; Dual-LUC; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold treatment at 4°C; MG132 and CHX treatments
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHOS1 interacts with both MdHB7L and MdICE1L, but prefers MdICE1L. 2. It ubiquitinates and degrades both proteins via the 26S proteasome pathway. 3. Cold inhibits MdHOS1-mediated MdHB7L ubiquitination/degradation. 4. MdHOS1 also reduces MdHB7L promoter binding and weakens transcriptional control of downstream metabolic genes. 5. Overexpression of MdHOS1 attenuates cold tolerance and reduces MdHB7L-mediated antioxidant and osmoprotective outputs.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher MdHOS1 activity reduces cold tolerance and beneficial metabolite accumulation under stress.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Applied species includes Arabidopsis for heterologous functional validation of cold sensitivity, but the source species is apple.
